scope_classification.label: local_experiment
scope_classification.rationale: The topic can be explored with small simulations, synthetic data, or lightweight local experiments on the Mac.
scope_classification.feasibility_note: The research topic on traceable autonomous research agents is feasible on your MacBook M4 with 36GB RAM, though the MPS GPU may limit training speed compared to dedicated GPUs. Suitable public datasets include the ArXiv academic paper dataset, Microsoft Academic Graph, and the CodeSearchNet dataset for code-based research capabilities. Expect initial experiments to take approximately 2-4 hours for smaller models and 8-12 hours for more complex architectures, with longer times for fine-tuning and

parsed_papers.title: The AI Scientist: Towards Fully Automated Open-Ended Scientific Discovery
parsed_papers.core_claim: The paper introduces an automated pipeline capable of performing the entire scientific discovery process, from generating ideas to drafting papers.
parsed_papers.possible_gap: A key gap is the development of robust and standardized evaluation frameworks for fully automated scientific discovery systems.

parsed_papers.title: The AI Scientist-v2: Workshop-Level Automated Scientific Discovery via Agentic Tree Search
parsed_papers.core_claim: The AI Scientist-v2 extends automated scientific discovery to the workshop level by introducing stronger search and a review-like iteration process with stricter quality gates.
limitations: The system's effectiveness is constrained by the difficulty of consistently meeting the stricter gates for novelty, experiment quality, and paper readiness.
parsed_papers.possible_gap: The paper opens the gap of how to effectively operationalize and automate the evaluation of novelty, experiment quality, and paper readiness at scale.

parsed_papers.title: Agent Laboratory: Using LLM Agents as Research Assistants
parsed_papers.core_claim: LLM agents can be used as research assistants to automate tasks like literature review, experimentation, and report writing.
method_or_focus: Development and application of the Agent Laboratory system
limitations: Requires significant human oversight and lacks inherent reproducibility controls.
parsed_papers.possible_gap: The paper does not detail how the system itself ensures reproducibility or minimizes the need for extensive human intervention.

claims.text: The paper introduces an automated pipeline capable of performing the entire scientific discovery process, from generating ideas to drafting papers.
The AI Scientist-v2 extends automated scientific discovery to the workshop level by introducing stronger search and a review-like iteration process with stricter quality gates.
LLM agents can be used as research assistants to automate tasks like literature review, experimentation, and report writing.

links.to: A key gap is the development of robust and standardized evaluation frameworks for fully automated scientific discovery systems.
The paper opens the gap of how to effectively operationalize and automate the evaluation of novelty, experiment quality, and paper readiness at scale.
The paper does not detail how the system itself ensures reproducibility or minimizes the need for extensive human intervention.
